D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

The Dress

toggle the assumed light and watch one set of pixels flip between white-gold and blue-black

To name a colour the brain must guess the light. Assume a cool blue light and it subtracts blue, reading white-gold; assume warm light and it subtracts warmth, reading blue-black.

The centre swatch holds the dress's actual, fixed pixels. Toggle the light your visual system assumes and watch the very same stripes resolve into two famously opposite dresses.
